nodes.message: Welcome to the NDS Client Management Playbook. What do you need help with?
What do you need help with for account setup and tools?

nodes.answer:
1. GM will request an ndsdata.com email from Kevin Deal.
2. TL/PM to track and follow up as needed.
3. Create a Google account using the format:
   firstname.lastname.ndsdata@gmail.com
Google Chat is the main communication channel for the CM team. Ask your leads to add you to the relevant CM spaces after your account is set up.

Airtable serves as the centralized database where the team inputs and tracks client information essential for generating accurate Estimations and Project Scoping Units (PSUs). Credentials will be provided by the CM leads upon onboarding.
Used to merge, compress, and split PSU files as needed for easier file management and submission.
Used to validate City, County, and State information for project locations. Go to the StatsAmerica website, provide the project state and city to confirm county details.

nodes.message: Google Earth Pro — what do you need help with?

nodes.answer:
Optimize performance by going to:
Tools > Options > Navigation

Use the 'Directions from here' or 'Directions to here' feature by right-clicking a pin in Places.

1. Right-click the closest pricing tier pin / office location → 'Directions from here'
2. Right-click the farthest project location pin → 'Directions to here'
3. Choose the first route result for fuel surcharge calculation and tier identification.

nodes.message: Google Earth Pro — Creating Folder Templates
nodes.answer:
1. In Places panel, right-click on My Places → Add > Folder.
2. Name the folder (e.g., 'Project Template').
3. Right-click the folder → Properties → Style, Color tab.
4. Set desired label color, icon, and label scale.
5. Click OK.
6. Add new features (placemarks) inside the folder — they inherit the folder's styles automatically.
7. To reuse: right-click folder → Save Place As (KML or KMZ).

nodes.message: Google Earth Pro — CSV Import & KST Style Template
nodes.answer:
1. Go to File > Import, select your CSV file.
2. Configure field types (Delimited, Comma) and map Latitude/Longitude columns.
3. When prompted 'Apply a style template?' → click Yes.
4. In Style Template Settings:
   - Name Tab: choose the column for placemark names
   - Color Tab: single color or set from field
   - Icon Tab: same icon or set from field
   - Height Tab: Clamp to ground
5. Click OK → Save the .kst file with a descriptive name.

Next time: choose 'Use existing template' during import.

Use cm@ndsdata.com for client communication.
Use your personal ndsdata email for internal responses.

Sync emails in Outlook manually through POP. Contact Paul Astrera for assistance.

nodes.message: NDS Standard Process Flow — from quotation to delivery:
nodes.answer:
1. Client requests quotation via email or phone call
2. Client Management → Mapping and Estimation
3. Area Manager sends estimate to client for approval
4. Client gives Notice to Proceed
5. Client Management generates a Cover Sheet (PSU)
6. Scheduling Team assigns schedule to technician
7. Technician installs camera/device
8. Technician tears down camera/device
9. Technician uploads file to server
10. Audit Team audits videos and checks for issues
11. Audit Team sends to JOBS EMAIL for video counting or finalizing
12. Area Manager — NDS delivers

⚠️ Redo/Re-installation: If Audit finds no upload, missing footage, or incomplete footage → sent back to Scheduling Team for re-assignment.

nodes.message: Email Request Review — what do you need help with?
Email Request Review — Step by Step
nodes.answer:
1. Receive client's request via cm@ndsdata.com inbox.

2. Record in CM Templates:
   Client Details:
   • Client name, Firm, Contact Number, Office Address

   Project Details:
   • Email subject
   • Request details (study type, collection days/duration, special requests)
   • Location info (region, region boundary, city, state, county)
   Note: Some locations are sent as attachments (map, list, PDF).

3. Create an <Estimate> folder in your Documents folder, then a subfolder with today's date <mm.dd.yyyy>. You may add a <1> subfolder for tasks of the day.

4. Drag the email into the working folder and save all attachments:
   • Client map
   • Client project locations list

Copy the full email thread into Gemini with this prompt:

'Analyze the email and list: Client Name, Company, Contact Number, Office Address, Email Subject, Study Type, Project Locations, Collection Time and Day, Number of Technicians.
• If sender is from NDSDATA.com, find the requester in the email and add to the list.
• If multiple locations, create CSV for TMC with: street, city, state. Replace "and" or "at" with "&".
• Study types: TMC or ATR.
• KMZ Filename format: (Client)-(Email Subject) (City, State Abbreviation)
• MAP Filename: MAP-<mm.dd.yyyy> <Client>-<Email Subject> <City, State>'

TMC Defaults:
• Duration: 4hrs (7:00-9:00AM & 4:00-6:00PM)
• Weekday: Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday
• Weekend: 3hrs (11:00AM-2:00PM)
• Camera installation needed for 9hrs+

ATR Defaults:
• Duration: 24 hours
• Collection day: Weekday
• Default add-on: Volume

nodes.message: Mapping — what do you need help with?

nodes.answer:
1. Open Google Earth (Desktop).
2. Under Temporary Places, create a new folder.
3. Name the KMZ file per naming rules.
4. Inside the folder, create one subfolder per study type (e.g., TMC).
5. Search for locations in the order provided by the client.
6. Add pins/polygons (proper colors & names). Refer to Study Data Bank for rules per study.
   a. If applicable, identify sites needing multiple persons/machines.
7. Save Place As (KMZ).

Format:
MAP-(mm.dd.yyyy) (Client)-(Email Subject) (City, State)

Notes:
• Do not repeat city/state if already included in the subject line.
• Use US date format (MM.DD.YYYY).
• Trim if the file name is too long.

Region-specific:
• South Central (TX/LA): Copy from Estimation Management → Proposal Line Items → Estimate Name. Replace 'ESTIMATE' with 'MAP'.
• Northeast/Mid-Atlantic: Label KMZ with the client's email subject (from Rachel). Avoid generic names like 'estimate request'.

nodes.message: Identifying Locations in Google Earth Pro
nodes.answer:
1. Search for the intersecting streets using ampersand (&) between road names.
   • Do not include 'city' or 'county' in names unless required.

2. Confirm the city:
   a. Click Google Maps icon → click map → see location address.
   b. If city is provided in the email, use that (even if Google differs).

3. Confirm the county:
   a. Go to StatsAmerica website.
   b. Provide the project state and city.
   c. You may also search via Google / Gemini.

Road Name Symbols:
• State Road → SR (e.g., SR 99)
• Country Road → CR (e.g., CR 455)
• US Highways → US (e.g., US 1)
• Interstate Highways → I (e.g., I 35W)

nodes.message: Measuring Distance for Fuel Surcharge / Tier Check
nodes.answer:
1. Zoom out in Google Earth Pro, ensure My Places is enabled. Make sure the National Pricing Map (updated 10.21.2025.kmz) is added.

2. Right-click the closest pricing tier pin → 'Directions from here'
3. Right-click the farthest project location pin → 'Directions to here'
4. Choose the first route result.

Fuel Surcharge by color zone:
• Green: 1-10 loc=$30, 11-19=$50, 20+=$70
• Yellow: 1-10 loc=$40, 11-19=$60, 20+=$80
• Red: 1-10 loc=$50, 11-19=$70, 20+=$80

Pin Color: Yellow
Pin Placement: Middle of intersection
Polygon: Yellow — Lines 3.0 width, 100% opacity; Area filled+outlined, 30% opacity
Path: None

Naming:
• Folder: TMC
• Default: TMC-001
• Add (# PERSON) if more than 1 person: e.g., TMC-001 (2 PERSON)
• Add (*-HR) if multiple durations: e.g., TMC-001 6HR

Person Count:
• ≤15 lanes → 1 person
• 16–22 lanes → 2 persons
• ≥23 lanes → 3 persons
• Add 1 person for cutouts (if road is large), RTOR (except FL), roundabouts

Polygon must include: pedestrian stoplines, cutouts, crosswalks, and sidewalks tightly with no extra spaces.

Pin Color: Red
• South Central with speed request: Green

Pin Placement: Middle of road, opposite a pole, signage, or fire hydrant. If none available, add ??? at the end of the pin name.
Polygon: None
Path: None

Naming:
• Folder: ATR
• TX & NoCal: ATR-001
• SoCal: ATR-001 24-HR VOLUME
• FL, SE, NE, Mid-Atlantic:
  ATR-001 24-HR VOLUME (1 MACHINE)
  ATR-001 24-HR VOLUME CLASS (2 MACHINES)
  ATR-001 24-HR VOLUME CLASS SPEED (1 MACHINE MULTICLASS)
  (and other combinations based on add-ons)

Number of Machines:
• Volume only → per direction (1-way=1, 2-way=2)
• With Class/Speed → per lane (excluding shared/turning lanes)
• With Class/Speed but 1 lane only → '1 machine multiclass'
• ATR approach → per leg

Pin Color: Sky Blue (VID) or Green (CAM)
Polygon: None
Path: Green (90° facing camera direction)

Pin Placement:
• VID: Middle of the road
• CAM: Side of the road, beside a post

Naming:
• VID-001
• CAM-001

Note: Replaces old ATR-camera.

Pin Color: Green
Pin Placement: Side of the road
Polygon: None
Path: None

Naming:
• Folder: RAD
• Pin: RAD-001
• Default 2 hours
• Regular intersection/segment: 1 tech

Pin Color: Yellow (Teal for South Central)
Polygon:
• South Central: No polygon
• NE/Mid-Atlantic: Yellow polygon (3.0 width, 100% opacity; 30% opacity area)
Path: None (Yellow polygon for NE/Mid-Atlantic only)
Pin Placement: Entrance (parking or driveway)
Camera Placement: Default 'No Camera Placement' for FL/SE

Naming:
• DWY-001 — if driveway study
• TGN-001 — if trip gen study

Pin Color: Pink (cams = Green; Blue for South Central)
Polygon: Pink (30% opacity) — must include crosswalks and sidewalks
Path: Green (90° facing camera)

Pin Placement:
• P&B pin: Middle of intersection
• CAM pin: Side of road, beside a post

Naming:
• Folder: P&B
• Pin: P&B-001
• Add (# PERSON) if more than 1 person
• Add (*-HR) if multiple durations
• Camera: CAM-001

Camera count:
• Regular intersection → 1 camera
• Large intersection (200ft+) → 2 cameras

Note: All regions except FL/SE allow cams.

Pin Color: Blue (cams = Green)
Polygon: None
Path: Green (90° facing camera)

Pin Placement:
• MNL pin: Middle or between roads
• CAM pin: Side of road, beside a post

Naming:
• Folder: MNL
• Pin: MNL-001
• Camera path: MNL-001

Important: All mainlines must have classification specified with FHWA breakdown.
If posted speed limit is 55mph, Wavetronix data and manual review are required.

Pin Color: Yellow (regular intersections) or Pink (zone)
Polygon: Yellow (regular) or Pink (zone) — 3.0 width, 100% opacity; 30% opacity
Path: None

Pin Placement: Middle of intersection/segment

Naming:
• Folder: PED
• Pin: PED-001
• Add (# PERSON) if more than 1 person
• Add (*-HR) if multiple durations

Camera count:
• Regular intersection → 1 camera
• Large intersection (200ft+) → 2 cameras

Polygon must include: stoplines, pedestrian lanes, sidewalks, and illegal crossings/jaywalking areas.

Pin Color: Any colored pin per site
Polygon: Same color as pin, filled and outlined — 3.0 width, 100% opacity; 30% opacity
Pin Placement: Anywhere within the site
Naming: PKG-001

Pin: Green
Polygon: None
Naming: QUE-001

Notes:
• NoCal & LA (South Central): map and price like LA Unmet Demand.
• NE/Mid-Atlantic: Create a queue and always put camera angle (200-300ft facing front of the vehicle).
• Be aware of road curves.
• Avoid placing cameras if there's a possible blockage (e.g., tree).
• SoCal Snapshot Queues can be done with 1 live technician.

Pin Color: Violet
Polygon: None
Naming: DRN-001

Pin: Any
Polygon: Path only
Naming: OSP-001

Guidelines:
• No need to break the path between store/residential driveways/entrances.
• Paths break on main streets ONLY and on big establishments ONLY IF THERE IS A STOPLIGHT.
• If there is an alley, confirm with the area manager if the path needs to break or continue as 1 OSP + path.
• Refer to RED LINE (updated) vs YELLOW LINE (old).

nodes.message: Estimation — what do you need help with?

nodes.answer:
1. Go to Airtable → login with CM team credentials.
2. Open Project Management file for the relevant region.
3. Go to Projects tab → ESTIMATES (Add/Edit) → Basic Details → click + to add one record per study type.

   Fill in:
   • Project Name = (Client)-(Email Subject) (City, State)
   • Region
   • Related Project (if any)
   • Client contact (verify email/firm matches signature)
   • Email CC (if any)
   • IPO (if any)
   • Project ID is auto-generated.

4. Go to Project Info view → fill in:
   • Locations, Service Type, Service Type Add Ons, City, County, State

5. Go to Client Attachments view → upload:
   • Client Map (screenshot via Win+Shift+S)
   • Client Locations List (screenshot if multiple)
   • Client template (if any)

6. OPTIONAL: If video delivery requested → enter in Video Details view:
   • Save Video? / Deliver Video? / Video Delivery Process / Notes

nodes.message: Estimate Record Creation — Estimation Management Airtable
nodes.answer:
1. Open Estimation Management file for the region.
2. Under Proposals tab → Pending Proposals - NEW view → click + to create a new record.
   Note: One record per project regardless of number of study types.
3. In Estimate IDs column → click + → Create a new record.
4. In the new record, supply:
   • Proposal # (always 1)
   • Project Name
   • Office Address
5. Note the Estimate ID under the Proposal column.

In Estimation Management → Proposal Line Items tab → Add/Edit Details - NEW view:

1. Create a new record → supply:
   • Proposal #, sProjects, Pricing Region, Service Types, sService Type AddOns
   • Pricing Model: always 'Custom'
   • Tier: always '1'
2. Check the 'Selections Complete' box.
3. Supply:
   • # Locations, # People (TMC), # Units (ATR), # Hours, # Days
   • Custom Pricing Method: always 'By Location'
4. In Custom Rate column, input calculation from Short Term Pricing Resource.
   Consider: number of days, hours, location tier, region, study type, add-ons.
5. Copy Rate Description column → paste in Custom Rate Description → delete '/WATCHES'.
6. Enter collection time, days, and additional info (camera install, specific dates, etc.).
7. If collection spans more than one day, revert # Days to '1' (Airtable auto-multiplies).
8. Repeat for multiple line items. Create separate items for different schedules, study types, add-ons, or person counts.
9. Note the Estimate Name in the last column.

⚠️ Always check Client's default notes and requirements before generating an estimate.

Create a new line item under the same proposal ID:

• sProjects (if multiple Project IDs, choose any)
• Pricing Region
• Service Types: always 'Fuel Surcharge'
• Pricing Model: always 'Custom'
• Tier: always '1'

Check 'Selections Complete' box, then supply:
• # Locations: 1
• # Days: 1
• Custom Pricing Method: By Location
• Custom Rate: (calculated or given fuel surcharge — minimum $25)
• Custom Rate Description: Fuel Surcharge

Note: No fuel surcharge if contract pricing is provided (including city contracts).

Print Estimate Sheet:
1. Click Extensions.
2. Search for the Estimate using the Estimate ID.
3. Check all details before printing.
   Note: If line items overlap page footers, use the second template.
4. Print.

Sending for Audit:
• Follow proper file name formats.
• Send: map (KMZ), estimate (PDF), and email (Outlook file).
• In Estimation Management Airtable, change Proposal Status from 'In Progress' to 'Pending Approval'.

Add Location Slots:
• Project Management → Locations tab → Add Locations and Date Ranges NEW view.
• Add as many records as there are locations.
• Fill in Location ID column with sequence numbers.

Change Proposal Status after sending map + estimate to CM gc for audit:
• Estimation Management → Proposals tab → Proposal Status → change to 'Proposal – Pending Approval'
• Attach KMZ: Project Management → Pending CM Proposal filter → Project Attachments → attach KMZ

File Path/Folder:
Region > Firm > (mm.dd.yyyy) (Client)-(Email Subject) (City, State)

KMZ File Name:
MAP-(mm.dd.yyyy) (Client)-(Email Subject) (City, State)

Project Name:
(Client)-(Email Subject) (City, State)

Estimate File Name:
ESTIMATE_(Firm)_(Client)_(CityState)_(yyyy-mm-dd)_v#

South Central (TX/LA) KMZ:
Copy from Estimation Management → Proposal Line Items → Estimate Name (last column). Change 'ESTIMATE' to 'MAP'.

nodes.message: Pricing — what do you need help with?

nodes.answer:
Florida, Southeast, Southcentral:
• Measure distance (miles) from closest pricing pin to farthest project location.
• Miami-Dade County, Palm Beach County, Broward County: always Tier 1.
• Gulf Coast/SW Florida counties (Charlotte, Collier, DeSoto, Glades, Hendry, Lee, Manatee, Sarasota): use the region boundary where the client's office address is.

NoCal, Northeast, Mid-Atlantic:
• Always given by the regional office.
• For NE and Mid-Atlantic: measure distance from closest pricing pin.

Minimum: $25
None if contract pricing is provided (including city contracts).
15+ locations: confirm number of techs.

Florida & Southeast:
(Mileage) × 2 × (# of trips) × (# of tech) × 0.13
*Round up to nearest $5

TMC trips (multiplier = days + 1):
• 1 day = mi × 2 × 2 × 0.13
• 2 days = mi × 2 × 3 × 0.13
• 3 days = mi × 2 × 4 × 0.13

ATR trips (multiplier = # of roundtrips: setup, teardown, checks):
• 1-2 days = mi × 2 × 2 × 0.13
• 3 days = mi × 2 × 3 × 0.13
• 4-7 days = mi × 2 × 4 × 0.13

Southcentral, Midwest & SoCal:
(Mileage) × 2 × (# of trips) × (# of tech) × 0.20
*Round up to nearest $5

NoCal: always given; no fuel surcharge if not given.

Normal TMC Pricing:
• 2-8 hours TMC
• Any duration with contract pricing

Camera Installation pricing:
• 9 hours and above TMC

Greater than 4 hours:
• Charge at 4-hour rate plus additional hour price

Pricing depends on: location (tier), duration (hours), person count.

Normal:
• 2 lane maximum roadway

Large:
• ≥4 lanes total (one way or bidirectional)
• Applicable to all regions except NorCal
• NE/Mid-Atlantic: Large = 3+ lanes

Freeway:
• High speed/heavy volume, meets safety requirements
• Installed with 2-man team
• Speed limit ≤55mph
• If 70-80mph: use Mainline study and install pricing

Camera Installation:
• Road makes machine installation impossible (e.g., ramps, bridge)
• Speed limit 70mph+

Video Review:
• Multiply number of hours × per hour pricing
• Add to camera installation price

Pricing depends on: location (tier + size), duration (days), add-ons.

• New client discount: 15%
• Volume discount: 10% (total amount > $10,000)

⚠️ All discounts require approval from Area Manager before applying.

Requires approval from Shen. If unavailable, ask Christine.

For 3 hours and below:
• Distance from Office (miles, one way): enter miles
• Distance from Hotel to Worksite: leave blank/0
• Nights at Hotel: leave blank/0
• No. of Hotels: leave blank/0
• No. of Vehicle Drivers: 1
• No. of Technicians: 1
• No. of Technician Full Working Days: leave blank/0
• Partial Working Day Hours: (hours × 4) + install/teardown hours

Example: 1 TMC 4hr 72.7mi 1hr15min
→ 1.25 × 4 = 5hr + 1hr install/teardown = 6hr partial working day hours

Note: Hotel usually applies if 3+ hours away.

nodes.message: PSU — what do you need help with?

nodes.answer:
KMZ: (Approved Estimate ID)_(City, State)_Map V1.0
Cover Sheet: (Approved Estimate ID)_(City, State)_Cover Sheet V1.0
Estimate: (Approved Estimate ID)_(City, State)_Estimate V1.0

Multiple versions: replace V1.0 with V1.1, V1.2…
Multiple project IDs: one cover sheet per project ID, but only one map and estimate with all project IDs in file names (separated by comma).

File Folders: Region > Date > (Approved Estimate ID)_(City, State)

The CM team receives PSU requests via email.

Note:
• PSU details (collection time, due date, hard due date, urgency)
• Location (region, region boundary, city, state)
• Estimate ID

Actions:
• Print email to PDF (block any pricing in the email)
• Download all attachments (map, estimate, client attachment, IPO)
• If client attachment is JPG/PNG → convert to PDF
• Find Project ID by searching Estimate ID in Estimation Management Airtable → Pending Proposals
• Double-check in Project Management Airtable → Basic Details, Project Info, Client Attachments, Video Details

In Project Management Airtable → Locations tab → Add Locations and Date Range:

Input:
• Location Type
• Cams (if any)
• Watches (if any)
• Watching Speed (if any)

Click + in date range column → create new record:
• Start Date
• End Date (same as start if 1 collection day)
• Collection Days (check calendar for day of week)
• Number of Days
• Collection Time (add new record if time not in options)

Copy the record to other locations with the same schedule/project number.
Create a new record for each variation of collection time, days, or add-ons.

In Project Management Airtable → Locations tab → Add Street Names:

• Input street names for each location.
• Input location notes in ALL CAPS if applicable:
  - 9HR, 2HR, 5HR → if multiple collection durations (use uncommon ones)
  - 2 PERSON → if multiple techs assigned
  - CAMERA INSTALLATION
  - VOLUME, CLASS, SPEED → always include for ATR/ADT
  - GPS Coordinates

In Project Management Airtable → Projects tab → Due Date Details:
• Due Date
• Hard Due Date (if urgency is 'Promised to Client')
• Urgency

In Project Notes view:
• Input project notes if any.
• Upload the map sent with the PSU request.
  Note: If a map was already uploaded, delete it and upload the most updated one.

1. Estimation Management Airtable → Proposals tab → change Proposal Status from 'Pending Approval' to 'Approved'

2. Project Management Airtable → Projects tab → Ready for Project Sequence ID view → in Project Sequence column, click + → type double spaces in the search bar → select the first result.
   Note: If the first result is '001', do not select — go to Approved Basic Details view and check the next sequence under the region boundary.

3. Note the Project Sequence ID from Estimation Management → Approved Proposals.

1. Project Management Airtable → Projects tab → click Extensions (top right).
2. Search for the Project Sequence ID generated.
   Note: Select 'All Projects' view or the project ID won't appear.
   Note: Customize pages (1-1, 1-2, etc.) depending on number of pages.
3. Merge the printed cover sheet with the client's attachment and printed email respectively.

1. Follow proper file name formats.
2. Double check everything before sending.
3. Send the following for audit:
   • Map (KMZ file)
   • Estimate (PDF file)
   • Email (Outlook file)
   • Merged cover sheet–attachment–PSU email (PDF file)
4. Add coordinates in Airtable under Location Tab.
5. Compress all PDF files before sending (use I PDF / PDF Combiner).

⚠️ Do NOT copy/paste details from other tabs — this merges data across projects.
⚠️ Always check the email thread for any rates or prices and redact them accordingly.

• 25-12xxxx — Tampa Region
• 25-13xxxx — Orlando Region

nodes.message: Study Types — which study do you need information about?

nodes.answer: Turning Movement Counts (TMC) collect data on the number of vehicles making specific turns (left, right, u-turn, or straight) at an intersection over a set period. Typically conducted during peak hours. TMC data is collected via camera/video recording.

• Default duration: 4 hours for AM and PM peak periods (7:00-9:00AM & 4:00-6:00PM)
• Default weekday collection: Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday
• Default weekend duration: 3 hours (11:00AM-2:00PM)

Rate Description Format:
Turning Movement Counts (1-Day, 4hr)
w/Pedestrians, Bicycles
1 PERSON COUNT(S)
7:00-9:00AM & 4:00-6:00PM
Weekday

Florida:
• Pedestrian, Bicycle, Heavy Trucks (FHWA 4+), Signal Phasing, Signal Timing, Sketch
• Kimley Horn Tampa & Plantation (Ian Rairden): always add RTOR

Southeast, NorCal:
• Pedestrian, Bicycle, Heavy Trucks (FHWA 4+)

Northeast, Mid-Atlantic:
• Pedestrian, Bicycle, Passenger Vehicle (FHWA 1-3), Heavy Trucks (FHWA 4+)
• Dolan & Dean, Gorove Slade: add Video Delivery

Southcentral, SoCal:
• Volume unless specified
• Carlson, Brigance & Doering (TX): Pedestrians, Bicycles, Heavy Trucks (FHWA 4+), Buses (All), Scooters
• WGI, Deshazo Group, TJKM: Pedestrians, Bicycles, Heavy Trucks

Common Other Add-ons:
• RTOR (signalized only; all FL locations regardless)
• U-Turns
• Buses (FHWA 4)
• Medium Trucks (FHWA 5-7)
• TTSTs/Combo Unit Trucks (FHWA 8-13)

3-bin Classification:
• Passenger Vehicles (FHWA 1-3)
• Duals/Single Unit Trucks (FHWA 4-7)
• Heavy Trucks (FHWA 8-13)

Automatic Traffic Recorder (ATR) is a device that automatically collects traffic data such as vehicle counts, classifications, and speeds. Installed along roadway segments. Data is summarized as Average Daily Traffic (ADT) — average vehicles passing a point over 24 hours. Also called tube counts or machine counts.

• Default duration: 24 hours
• Default collection day: Weekday
• Default add-on: Volume

Rate Description Format:
Machine Count (1-Day, 24hr)
w/Volume, Class, Speed
Weekday

For multiple days: Machine Count (2-Day, 48hr) — note total days and total hours.

All regions (FL, SE, SoCal, NorCal, Southcentral, NE, Mid-Atlantic):
• Volume (default)

Other add-ons as requested:
• Speed
• Classification

Definition: Collects accurate and reliable data on vehicle speed.

Default Rate Description:
Radar (Spot Speed) Study (1-Day, 2hr)
100 samples total or 2hr max per Interval (whichever comes first)
Weekday

SoCal & NoCal add: 9:00AM-4:00PM to the rate description.

Other regions: Up to 125 samples (refer to client request).

Pricing: Radar Average Volume (1 Hour) as default.
SoCal: Low Volume Rate applies.

Common add-ons:
• 100 samples per location per direction (default)
• 200 samples per location per direction
Collection window: 9AM-4PM

Definition: Collects total volume counts of all vehicle types entering/exiting an establishment (parking lot, private building, gas station, etc.). Trip Generation tracks vehicles going IN vs. OUT.

Default:
• No default collection time (use same as related study if applicable)
• Default day: Weekday

Default Add-ons:
• w/In & Out Volume Count (unless client requests specific classification)
• w/Camera Installation (if more than 8hrs)

Pricing:
• 8hrs and below: charge as TMC pricing
• Above 8hrs: Camera Installation + Video Review (usually 4x speed)

Notes per region if applicable.

Definition: Collects pedestrian data including crossings, sidewalk activity per direction & per side of street, and jaywalking.

Default collection day: Weekday

Common Add-ons:
• Crosswalk
• Screenline (sidewalk activity per direction & per side)
• Sidewalk Activity (per direction)
• Jaywalking
• Age (Adults vs. Elderly vs. School-Aged)
• Corner Activity (PED TMC)
• In-line Skaters
• Per Movement Grouping
• Stroller Use, Trail Count, Walking Speed Timing
• Signal Actuation/Activation

Pricing:
• Same as TMC pricing (tier, duration, person count)
• 9hrs+: Camera Installation + Video Review
• 8hrs and below: regular TMC pricing

Definition: Collects data on pedestrians and bicycles at an intersection over a set period. Data collected via camera/video recording.

Default collection day: Weekday

Common Add-ons:
• Crosswalk, Screenline, Sidewalk Activity, Jaywalking
• Age (Adults vs. Elderly vs. School-Aged)
• Corner Activity, In-line Skaters
• Per Movement Grouping, Stroller Use, Trail Count
• Walking Speed Timing, Signal Actuation/Activation

Pricing:
• Depends on tier, duration, person count
• 9hrs+: Camera Installation + Video Review

Definition: Collects data on pedestrians and bicycles making specific turns at an intersection/segment over a set period. Data collected via camera/video recording.

Default duration: 4 hours (7:00-9:00AM & 4:00-6:00PM)
Default collection day: Weekday

Default Add-ons (all regions):
• Pedestrian, Bicycle, Crosswalk, Sidewalk Activity

Pricing: Camera Installation pricing & video review from Short Term Pricing Resource. Depends on tier, duration, person count. Watch speed varies by location size.

Definition: Measures the range/distance between the rear of a subject vehicle and the front bumper of the following vehicle in the target lane. A primary safety measure for lane changes.

Default duration: 4 hours (7:00-9:00AM & 4:00-6:00PM)
Default collection day: Weekday

Common Add-ons:
• Per Both Directions Combined
• Per Direction
• Per Direction and Per Both Directions Combined

Pricing:
• From Short Term Pricing Resource (tier, duration, person count)
• 9hrs+: Camera Installation + Video Review

Northeast/Mid-Atlantic:
• Gap with provided video: $100 for 1st hour, $35 for each subsequent hour
• Regular Gap Study: Camera Installation with regular speed
• Gap studies made for proposed roads (e.g., future driveways)

Definition: Collects data on the number of vehicles on a roadway segment.

Default duration: 4 hours (7:00-9:00AM & 4:00-6:00PM)
Default weekday: Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday
Default weekend: 3 hours (11:00AM-2:00PM)

Default Add-ons: Same as TMC by region.
Common Other Add-ons: Same as TMC.
3-bin classification: Same as TMC.

Pricing: From Short Term Pricing Resource. Depends on tier, duration, person count. 9hrs+: Camera Installation + Video Review.

⚠️ All mainlines must specify classification with FHWA breakdown. If posted speed is 55mph, Wavetronix data and manual review required.

Definition: Analyzes buildup, length, and behavior of vehicle queues at intersections, toll booths, or bottlenecks. Examines wait times, queue length, arrival patterns, and service rates.

NoCal & LA (South Central): Map and price like LA Unmet Demand.

Pricing varies by region:
• NE/Mid-Atlantic: Camera at 200-300ft facing front of vehicle, reviewed at 2x speed
• NoCal: $75 × number of hours; Initial Queue = standard; Regular Queue = additional camera; Drive-thru = live tech
• SoCal: Snapshot with 1 live tech; 5-min increments; $15 every hr of processing; 1 tech = 1 drive-thru

Definition: Collects data on vehicles unable to pass a green light (demand that exceeds capacity).

Applicable regions: TX, LA, NE, Mid-Atlantic

Default Rate Description:
Unmet Demand Study (1-Day, 4hr)
1 signalized Intersection
- 15 Min Intervals (per approach)
- # of vehicles queued
- length/landmark-based queue if queue is too long
* 3 Cameras
07:00-09:00 & 16:00-18:00 | WKDY

Pricing:
(Video watching rate × hours × cameras) ÷ 4x speed + extra camera installs
Example: $35 × 4hrs = $140 × 3 cameras = $420 ÷ 4 = $105 + $205 + $205 = $515
TMC cam: charge video review only. Other 2 cams: charge video review + camera installation, all at 4x speed.

Definitions:
• Off-Street Parking (PKG-001): Parking within an establishment or structure.
• On-Street Parking (OSP-001): Parking along a street, segment, or open lot.
• Parking Occupancy: How many spaces are occupied + last 4 digits of license plate.
• Parking Turnover: How long each vehicle was parked (duration).
• Parking Inventory: How many spaces are available (20-22ft per space, always included in pricing — NON-NEGOTIABLE).

Fuel Surcharge for Parking:
• Regular: Fuel = mileage × 2 × 2 trips × (# tech) × 0.13
• If collection >8hrs, technician count doubles for fuel calculation.
• Parking fuel always computed separately from other studies.

Off-Street Parking:
1. Create the inventory path — ensure efficient route with least overlap.
2. Apply 20% reduction to mileage for occupancy path.
   Example: 2.50 miles − 20% = 2.00 miles (use for occupancy)
3. Use 2.50 miles to get Suggested Inventory Hours from pricing sheet.
4. Technician capacity: 75% work / 25% break (45 min work + 15 min rest per hour).

For Peak Hour Studies:
• Use full collection window (60 min) for hourly sweeps to determine tech count.
• Technician can rest between peak periods.

For On-Street Parking:
• No 20% reduction applied.
• Use normal path mileage for both inventory and occupancy.

Tech Count Formula:
• Continuous (e.g., 7AM-6PM): 45 min baseline → if time slightly exceeds, 1 tech may still suffice
• Peak hours: 60 min baseline → any sweep under 60 min requires only 1 tech for hourly sweeps
• Interval calculation: suggested minutes ÷ sweep interval = # techs needed

Collects aerial imagery for traffic and site analysis.

Mapping: Violet pin, no polygon. Naming: DRN-001.

Refers to parking space along the street, segment, or open lot.

Mapping: Path only (no polygon fill).
Naming: OSP-001

Guidelines:
• No need to break path at store/residential driveways/entrances.
• Break paths at main streets ONLY and at big establishments with a stoplight.
• If alley: confirm with area manager whether to break or continue with 1 OSP + path.
• Refer to RED LINE (updated) vs YELLOW LINE (old).

• File name format
• Location (if unmatched between client list and client map → inform area manager and ask which to use)
• Person count
• Cut out

• File name
• Logo
• Client's name and office address
• City / Job Ref
• IPO (if any)
• Rate description
• Study type & format
• # of Days & Hours
• Classification
• Standard add-ons (check Updates per Region tab)

• Person count

• Tier

• Price — manual computation
• Area Manager's email and local office address

  - New client: 15%
  - Volume: 10% (total > $10k)

Check the following:
• Extra spaces and/or punctuation
• Misspelling/typographical errors
• Custom ID
• PSU version
• IPO number
• City, County, or State
• Client attachment
• Location notes
• Location naming
• Redact pricing
• Print format
• Merging order
• Incorrect file attachment
• Missing client contact number
• Collection time
• Location quantity
• Client name
• Client firm and office address
• Service type
• Due date and urgency
• Number of days
• Add-ons
• Client map attachment
• Video requirements
• Email to Area Template/Attachments
• Collection day
• Orientation

Minor Error (-1):
• Map file name format/version, extra spaces, misspelling, study type folder
• Estimate: extra spaces, misspelling, estimate version, rate order of line items, missing project name, missing client contact number
• PSU: extra spaces, misspelling, custom ID, PSU version
• Audit: screenshot conversation

Major Error (-5):
• Map: pin/polygon color, pin naming, machine count, city/county/state, person counts, street view pinning, print format
• Estimate: IPO number, city/county/state, local/remittance address, outdated contact, project name, rate description format, CC and client email, merge format, file name PSU
• PSU: IPO, city/county/state, client attachment, location notes/naming, redact pricing, print format, merging order, incorrect attachment
• Audit: status update

Auto Fail (-100):
• Map: incorrect/missing location, incorrect ATR add-on naming, incorrect collection hours in pin, polygon/path coverage, region boundary, order of locations, client's requests
• Estimate: collection time, location quantity, client name, firm/office address, service type, fuel surcharge, discount, region code/project number, number of days, add-ons, video requirements, logo, person count, region/client standard, year
• PSU: incorrect/missing location, pricing, project notes, collection time, client form/office address, service type, due date, urgency, number of days, add-ons, client map attachment, video requirements, email to area, collection day, orientation, number sequencing, project attachment, year
